networkStats.title: Network Stats
networkStats.subtitleTestnet: Stats are occasionally reset on testnet.

table.statusTooltip: The finality status of the block
table.batchTooltip: The batch index where the block transactions are submitted to L1
table.timestampTooltip: The date and time at which a block is mined
table.blockNumberTooltip: Block height, indicates the length of the blockchain, increases after the addition of the new block
table.blockSizeTooltip: Number of transactions inside the block
table.blockHashTooltip: The hash of the current block
table.commitTxHashTooltip: Hash of the L1 transaction sent to the smart contract to commit the block
table.committedAtTooltip: Time when the block was committed
table.proveTxHashTooltip: Hash of the L1 transaction in which block was proven
table.executeTxHashTooltip: Hash of the L1 transaction in which block was executed
table.notFoundHomePage: We haven't had any blocks yet. Please, check again later.

transactionTable.title: Block Transactions
transactionTable.noTransactions: This Block doesn't have any transactions
transactionTable.blockNotFound: This Block has not been created or sealed yet

blocks.tooltipInfo: Latest blocks produced by the network

transfers.notFound: Address doesn't have any transfers at this moment

table.reasonTooltip: The revert reason of the transaction
table.errorTooltip: The error message of the transaction
table.transactionHashTooltip: Transaction hash is a unique 66-character identifier that is generated whenever a transaction is executed
table.nonceTooltip: Number of transactions sent from a sender address
table.timestampTooltip: The date and time at which a transaction is produced
table.fromTooltip: The sending party of the transaction
table.toTooltip: The receiving party of the transaction
table.tokensTransferredTooltip: List of tokens transferred within this transaction
table.blockTooltip: Number of the block in which the transaction is recorded
table.batchTooltip: The batch index where this transaction is submitted to L1
table.valueTooltip: Amount of Ether being transferred from one address to another within a transaction.
table.feeTooltip: Fee which sender paid for this transaction, amount in chosen asset & price in USD at the current time
table.contractAddressTooltip: The receiving party of the transaction (could be a contract address)
table.inputDataTooltip: Additional data included for this transaction. Commonly used as part of contract interaction or as a message sent to the recipient.
table.notYetSentOnTheChain: Not yet sent on the chain
table.notFoundHomePage: We haven't had any transactions yet. Please, check again later.
table.gasLimitAndUsedTooltip: Maximum amount of gas allocated for the transaction & the amount eventually used.
table.gasPerPubdataTooltip: Maximum amount of gas the user is willing to pay for a single byte of published data (pubdata).

errors.signature_decode_limited: Contract not verified - showing method signature only (parameter values unavailable)

batches.title: Batches
batches.indexTooltip: The L1 Batch number indicates the length of L1 Batches produced by grouping L2 blocks to be proven on Ethereum
batches.sizeTooltip: Number of transactions inside of the L1 Batch
batches.timestampTooltip: The date and time at which L1 Batch is produced
batches.rootHashTooltip: The L1 Batch Root is a cryptographic hash (or Merkle root) that summarizes a batch of L2 transactions or data and is submitted to the L1 blockchain as a single transaction
batches.commitTxHashTooltip: Hash of L1 transaction on which this L1 Batch was committed
batches.committedAtTooltip: The date and time at which L1 Batch is committed to Ethereum
batches.proveTxHashTooltip: Hash of L1 transaction on which this L1 Batch was proven
batches.provenAtTooltip: The date and time at which L1 Batch is proven to Ethereum
batches.executeTxHashTooltip: Hash of L1 transaction on which this L1 Batch was executed
batches.executedAtTooltip: The date and time at which L1 Batch is executed on Ethereum
batches.notYetSealed: This batch is not sealed yet

contractVerification.title: Smart Contract Verification
contractVerification.subtitle: Source code verification provides transparency for users interacting with smart contracts. By uploading the source code, BattleChain will match the compiled code with that on the blockchain.

resources.title: You can also verify your smart-contract using {hardhat}

contractAddress.underline: The 0x address supplied on contract creation.

validation.not_valid: Please, provide a valid contract address

contractName.label: Contract Name
contractName.solcUnderline: Must match the name specified in the code. For example, in contract MyContract { '{..}' } MyContract is the contract name.
contractName.vyperUnderline: Must match the filename. For example, in contract MyContract.vy MyContract is the contract name.

contractPath.label: Contract Path
contractPath.underline: Relative path to your contract at the moment of compilation

runs.label: Runs
runs.underline: Optimize for how many times you intend to run the code. Lower values will optimize more for initial deployment cost, higher values will optimize more for high-frequency usage.

optimizationUsed.label: Optimization
optimizationUsed.underline: If you enabled optimization during compilation, select yes.

safeHarbor.anonymousAllowedTooltip: No identity verification required to claim bounty
safeHarbor.identityPseudonymousTooltip: A handle or alias is required to claim bounty
safeHarbor.identityNamedTooltip: Full identity verification required to claim bounty
safeHarbor.noAgreementSimple: This contract does not have a registered Safe Harbor Agreement.
safeHarbor.noAgreementMessage: This contract does not have a registered Safe Harbor Agreement. Default rescue terms from the network may still apply.
safeHarbor.defaultTermsNote: These are the default terms enforced by the network for contracts without a registered agreement.
safeHarbor.termsCanChange: Protocol can modify terms at any time

edit.bountyPercentageError: Bounty percentage must be between 1 and 100
edit.bountyCapError: Please enter a valid bounty cap
edit.diligencePlaceholder: Describe diligence requirements for named rescuers...
edit.aggregateCapHelp: Maximum total bounties across all rescues (optional)
edit.invalidAddress: Please enter a valid Ethereum address
edit.agreementURIHelp: Link to the full legal agreement document (IPFS, Arweave, or HTTP)
edit.commitmentWindowNote: During the commitment window, only whitehat-favorable changes are allowed.
edit.lockedRestrictions: Terms are locked. You can only make whitehat-favorable changes: increase bounties, loosen identity requirements, add covered contracts, or extend the commitment window.
edit.cannotDecreaseWhenLocked: Cannot decrease when terms are locked
edit.cannotRemoveWhenLocked: Cannot remove contracts when terms are locked
edit.cannotRestrictWhenLocked: Cannot make identity requirements stricter when locked
edit.extendDeadlineHelp: Must be later than the current deadline
edit.deadlineMustBeAfterCurrent: New deadline must be after the current deadline

registered.title: Registered
registered.description: This contract is protected by the standard SEAL Whitehat Safe Harbor Agreement, which protects whitehats who intervene during active exploits by malicious actors. This contract is NOT a legal target for proactive security testing until the owner requests attack mode.

pendingApproval.title: Pending Approval
pendingApproval.description: This contract is protected by the standard SEAL Whitehat Safe Harbor Agreement, which protects whitehats who intervene during active exploits by malicious actors. This contract is NOT a legal target for proactive security testing until the DAO approves attack mode.

active.title: Active Protection
active.description: This contract is currently in attack mode. Whitehats are protected under these Safe Harbor terms when conducting authorized security testing.

promotionPending.title: Promotion Pending
promotionPending.description: The owner has requested promotion to production. This contract remains attackable during the 3-day promotion window.

production.title: Production Mode
production.description: This contract has been promoted to production and is no longer open for attack mode testing. For responsible disclosure or bug bounty inquiries, contact the protocol using the details below.

corrupted.title: Compromised
corrupted.description: This contract was marked as compromised following a successful attack. The Safe Harbor agreement terms may apply for asset recovery.

childScope.existingOnlyTooltip: Includes child contracts that existed when the agreement was created
childScope.allTooltip: Includes all child contracts created by this contract (past and future)
childScope.futureOnlyTooltip: Only includes child contracts created after the agreement

createAgreement.title: Create Safe Harbor Agreement
createAgreement.toCreateIt: to set up a Safe Harbor Agreement.
createAgreement.notOwner: Your connected wallet is not the deployer of this contract. Only the contract deployer can create a Safe Harbor Agreement.
createAgreement.retainable: Allow whitehats to retain recovered funds
createAgreement.retainableHint: If enabled, whitehats may keep a portion of recovered funds as their bounty
createAgreement.diligencePlaceholder: Describe any additional requirements for named rescuers...
createAgreement.aggregateCapHint: Maximum total bounties across all rescues (optional)
createAgreement.recoveryAddressHint: Address where recovered assets will be sent
createAgreement.coveredContractHint: This contract will be covered by the Safe Harbor Agreement
createAgreement.coveredContractsHint: Contracts covered by this Safe Harbor Agreement. Scope determines if child contracts are included.
createAgreement.agreementURIHint: Link to the full legal agreement document (optional)
createAgreement.adoptDescription: Optionally register this agreement for your wallet in the BattleChain Safe Harbor Registry.
createAgreement.adoptOptionalNote: This step is optional. You can skip it if you only need the agreement contract.
createAgreement.completeDescription: Your agreement is now registered in the Safe Harbor Registry.
createAgreement.completeCreatedOnlyDescription: Your agreement contract is ready. You can adopt it in the registry later if needed.

errors.bountyPercentageInvalid: Bounty percentage must be between 1 and 100
errors.contactRequired: At least one contact method is required

contractRegistration.description: This contract is not registered in the Attack Registry. Unregistered contracts may be registered at any time and could request to enter the "under attack" state.
contractRegistration.warning: Consider this when deciding whether to interact with or deposit funds into this contract.
contractRegistration.notOwner: Your connected wallet is not the deployer of this contract. Only the contract deployer can register it.
contractRegistration.requestAttackableDescription: Request Attack Mode - Submit a request for this contract to enter attackable mode. Requires DAO approval since this contract wasn't deployed via BattleChainDeployer. Once approved, ethical hackers can test this contract under Safe Harbor protection.

contractNewDeployment.title: Registered
contractNewDeployment.description: This contract is registered in the Attack Registry. You can request Attackable Mode to allow ethical hackers to test your contract under Safe Harbor protection, or skip testing and go straight to production.
contractNewDeployment.notOwner: Only the contract deployer can request Attackable Mode or go to production.
contractNewDeployment.goToProductionLink: Skip testing? Go straight to production

goToProduction.title: Go to Production
goToProduction.warningDescription: Going straight to production means your contract will not go through the Attackable Mode security testing phase. This action cannot be undone.
goToProduction.contractsAffectedTitle: {count} contract will be promoted | {count} contracts will be promoted
goToProduction.contractsAffectedDescription: Going to production will promote the following contracts, skipping the attack testing phase.
goToProduction.successDescription: Your contract has been moved to production mode and is no longer available for ethical hacking.

requestPromotion.title: Request Promotion
requestPromotion.warningDescription: Requesting promotion will start a 3-day waiting period. During this time, your contract remains attackable and whitehats can continue testing. After the delay, your contract will move to Production.
requestPromotion.contractsAffectedTitle: {count} contract will be promoted | {count} contracts will be promoted
requestPromotion.contractsAffectedDescription: The following contracts will exit Attackable mode after the 3-day delay.
requestPromotion.successDescription: Your contract will move to Production in 3 days. During this time, it remains in Attackable mode and whitehats can continue testing.

cancelPromotion.title: Cancel Promotion
cancelPromotion.infoDescription: Canceling the promotion will return your contract to Attackable mode. Whitehats will be able to continue testing your contract under Safe Harbor protection.
cancelPromotion.successDescription: Your contract has returned to Attackable mode and is open for ethical hacking.

requestAttackableMode.title: Request Attackable Mode
requestAttackableMode.description: Ready to stress test your contract? Request Attackable Mode to allow ethical hackers to test your contract under the protection of your Safe Harbor agreement. Your request will be reviewed by the DAO. Your agreement must have a commitment window of at least 7 days to ensure whitehats have stable terms.
requestAttackableMode.notOwner: Only the contract deployer can request Attackable Mode.
requestAttackableMode.success: Attackable Mode requested! Your request will be reviewed by the DAO.

requestUnderAttackModal.title: Request Attackable Mode
requestUnderAttackModal.noAgreementsFound: No agreements found covering this contract
requestUnderAttackModal.waitingForDetection: Waiting for it to be detected...
requestUnderAttackModal.completeDescription: Your request will be reviewed by the DAO.
requestUnderAttackModal.invalidAddress: Please enter a valid Ethereum address
requestUnderAttackModal.connectWalletPrompt: Connect your wallet to submit the request
requestUnderAttackModal.contractsAffectedTitle: {count} contract will be affected | {count} contracts will be affected
requestUnderAttackModal.contractsAffectedDescription: Requesting attackable mode will upgrade the following contracts to the attack requested state.

contractState.autoPromotionTooltip: If no attack is registered before {date}, this contract will automatically be promoted to production.
contractState.termsLockedTooltip: The Safe Harbor Agreement terms are locked and cannot be modified during this window. This protects rescuers by ensuring the bounty terms remain stable.

debuggerTool.title: zkEVM Debugger
debuggerTool.whatFor: With this tool you can replay execution of your smart-contracts, and read each instruction
debuggerTool.unableToParseTrace: Sorry, we couldn't parse this trace file

systemAlert.indexerDelayedDueToHeavyLoad: The network is under a heavy load at the moment and transaction indexing on the explorer is {indexerDelayInHours} hours behind. Transactions are being processed normally and will gradually show up.

walletInfoModal.wrongNetwork: Your wallet activated an incorrect network
